model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Houston's elite defensive pressure and superior interior presence, led by their top-5 adjusted defensive efficiency, provide a significant advantage against an Illinois squad that relies heavily on its nation-leading offense. While the Illini possess elite shot-making and transition play, the loss of key guard Kylan Boswell to a broken hand compromises their backcourt depth and ability to handle the Cougars' relentless physical pressure. Expect Houston to control the tempo and dominate the glass, ultimately stifling Illinois' high-octane attack in a low-possession, half-court battle.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois vs. Houston Sweet 16 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: A matchup between the nation's #1 offense (Illinois) and #4 defense (Houston), with Houston holding a slight analytical edge and Illinois facing backcourt depth issues due to injury.
evidence: Houston ranks 5th in both KenPom and Bart Torvik, boasting the #4 adjusted defensive efficiency and the #13 adjusted offensive efficiency.
Illinois ranks 7th in KenPom and 6th in Torvik, with the #1 adjusted offensive efficiency but a lower-ranked defense at #24.
Illinois is confirmed to be without guard Kylan Boswell (broken hand), which impacts their ball-handling against Houston's pressure.
Houston's slow-paced, physical style often prevails in high-stakes tournament settings compared to high-tempo offenses.
